title: Bothriocline calycina
intro: Bothriocline calycina, also known as Indian sage, is a perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bothriocline calycina include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, decoction, or powder, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and stress-related disorders.
taxonomy: Bothriocline calycina is classified under the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Bothriocline, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical Africa. The species Bothriocline calycina is further classified as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of plants that are characterized by their small, daisy-like flowers. Within the Senecioneae tribe, Bothriocline calycina is placed in the subtribe Senecioninae, which includes a wide range of plants with similar characteristics. This classification helps to organize and understand the relationships between different plant species.
